items.q: Asch's line study primarily demonstrates which type of conformity?
items.correct: Normative social influence (compliance to fit in).
items.misconception: Informational social influence (believing others know better).
items.why: Students assume participants genuinely believed the wrong line was correct. Asch's debriefs showed they knew it was wrong but conformed publicly to avoid rejection - this is normative, not informational.

items.q: Which component handles visual and spatial information?
items.why: Students mix up the slave systems. The phonological loop is for auditory/verbal information; the sketchpad is for visual/spatial. The central executive coordinates but doesn't store.

items.q: According to Maslow, when can self-actualisation be achieved?
items.correct: Only after lower deficiency needs are largely met.
items.misconception: Anyone can self-actualise at any time through willpower.
items.why: Students miss the hierarchical structure. You cannot pursue growth needs while physiological, safety, love, and esteem needs are unmet - that's the whole point of a hierarchy.

items.q: Maguire (2000) found enlarged ___ in London taxi drivers:
items.why: Correct answer: Hippocampi. “Amygdala” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.
Correct answer: 5-HTT and depression. “DRD4 and risk-taking” sounds right because it borrows the language of the topic — but it fails this question. Re-read the notes on why.

items.q: In what year did Maguire publish her London taxi drivers study?
items.why: The real figure is 2000 AD. The wrong options cluster deliberately close — anchor the exact number to one concrete fact so it can't drift.
